name: Salmon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g edible-flesh basis keeps the entry aligned with a base whole-food ingredient rather than a cooked preparation.

metricProvenance.header: USDA FoodData Central style raw Atlantic-salmon entry, normalized to per 100 g edible portion
metricProvenance.saturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.omega3_mg: derived fatty-acid field from the same source family and directionally central to why salmon scores well
metricProvenance.polyunsaturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.cholesterol_mg: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.collagen_g: current value is still a rough calibration-era proxy, not a locked salmon-cut-specific source field
metricProvenance.essential_amino_acids_score: current value is still a calibration proxy rather than an exact-source amino-acid profile for this chosen salmon form
metricProvenance.nonessential_amino_acids_score: current value is still a calibration proxy rather than an exact-source amino-acid profile for this chosen salmon form
metricProvenance.bioavailability_percent: current value is still a proxy estimate, not a locked canonical percent for this exact salmon entry
metricProvenance.vitamin_a_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_c_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_d_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_e_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_k_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_b12_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.iron_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.magnesium_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.zinc_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.calcium_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.potassium_dv: same canonical source family as header

pros.title: easy to build a strong meal around
pros.explanation: It works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses.

pros.title: very satisfying as a protein source
has unusually strong trust with viewers
pros.explanation: People already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

cons.title: quality varies with sourcing
cons.explanation: Wild vs farmed differences matter in practice.

cons.title: needs good storage and handling
cons.explanation: A practical downside compared with some shelf-stable foods.

scoreReadiness.summary: Strong enough for launch scripting as a raw salmon baseline, but a few protein-quality style fields are still legacy calibration proxies and salmon source variation can move the fat profile.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
wild-versus-farmed and species variation can shift the exact fat and omega-3 profile meaningfully

sourceNotes: Identity locked to raw salmon flesh, using Atlantic salmon style per-100 g baseline values.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style raw Atlantic salmon entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked salmon values.
